subject: Physics
title: Quantum Cryptography
description: Learn Quantum Cryptography in one minute.

hook.text: Quantum cryptography creates codes that are impossible to crack—guaranteed by the laws of physics.

buildup.text: In quantum key distribution, two parties share a key using entangled or single photons.

discovery.text: If anyone intercepts the photons, the measurement disturbs them. The eavesdropper is always detected.

twist.text: China launched a quantum satellite in 2016 and demonstrated secure communication over 1,200 kilometers.

climax.text: As quantum computers threaten current encryption, quantum cryptography may become essential for security.

punchline.text: Tap the line and physics catches you. Unbreakable by law.

quiz.question: Why is quantum cryptography theoretically unbreakable?
options: Intercepting quantum signals disturbs them, revealing the eavesdropper
Entangled particles destroy themselves when observed
